question_title: How many problems solved by AI will the official FrontierMath Open Problems index show on May 1, 2026?
summary: Currently 1 FrontierMath Open Problem is solved (Ramsey Hypergraphs, solved March 23, 2026 by GPT-5.4 Pro). With only ~20 days until the May 1 resolution date, the status quo of 1 solved problem is heavily favored. The Small Diophantine problem has partial progress (3/9 equations) but the remaining 6 require "a genuinely new mathematical approach." Most remaining problems are in harder tiers that have resisted all AI attempts. The Poisson base rate (~0.1 solves/week, λ=0.3 for 3 weeks) gives ~74% chance of no new solve, consistent with my estimate. The main upside risk is a new frontier model release or an unexpected breakthrough on a Moderately interesting problem.

factors.description: Status quo: Currently 1 problem solved (Ramsey Hypergraphs). If nothing changes, the count stays at 1.
Small Diophantine has partial progress (3/9 equations) but remaining 6 need 'genuinely new mathematical approach' - unlikely to be fully solved in 20 days

Multiple frontier AI models (GPT-5.4 Pro, Opus 4.6, Gemini 3.1 Pro) are actively being tested on these problems, and AI math capabilities are advancing rapidly (2% to 50% on FrontierMath Tiers 1-4 in ~18 months)
Most remaining problems are in harder tiers (Solid result, Major advance, Breakthrough) - only 3 unsolved 'Moderately interesting' problems remain, and these already resisted GPT-5.4 Pro attempts
Short time window: only ~20 days until May 1 resolution. Even if a solution is found, verification by problem contributors and update to the website takes time.
No new model releases or breakthroughs announced in the last 2-3 weeks. The last solve (Ramsey Hypergraphs) was March 23, nearly 3 weeks ago with no additional solves since.
Manifold market for full-year 2026 gives ~15% to just 1 solve, meaning most traders expect more than 1 by EOY. But May 1 is much sooner than EOY, limiting the window for additional solves.
factors.conditional: If Manifold is well-calibrated for full year

factors.description: Possibility of a new frontier model release (e.g., GPT-5.5, Claude 5, Gemini 4) in the next 20 days that could crack additional problems

reasoning:
## Forecast Summary

**Question:** How many FrontierMath Open Problems will be shown as AI-solved on May 1, 2026?

| Outcome | Probability |
|---------|------------|
| **1 or fewer** | **70%** |
| **2** | **22%** |
| **Greater than 2** | **8%** |

**Key reasoning:**

- **Current state:** 1 problem solved (Ramsey Hypergraphs, March 23 by GPT-5.4 Pro) out of 15 total problems across 4 difficulty tiers
- **Partial progress:** Small Diophantine has 3/9 equations solved but remaining 6 need "a genuinely new mathematical approach" — not close to a full solve
- **Time constraint:** Only ~20 days remain, and no new solves have occurred in the 19 days since the Ramsey solve
- **Difficulty gradient:** Most remaining problems are in harder tiers (Solid result, Major advance, Breakthrough) that have resisted all AI attempts
- **Base rate:** ~0.1 solves/week → Poisson λ=0.3 for 3 weeks gives ~74% chance of no new solve, aligning with the 70% estimate
- **Upside risk:** Rapid AI capability advancement and active testing by multiple labs create meaningful (~22%) probability of a second solve, but >2 solves would require extraordinary circumstances

resolution_criteria: This question resolves as the total number of  AI-solved problems shown by the official FrontierMath Open Problems index shows on May 1, 2026. https://epoch.ai/frontiermath/open-problems/
